rules.title: Game Overview
rules.content: RUPANTOR is a transformation-based card game where players transform the current World State into a new state by playing cards whose category matches the World State's transformation paths.

rules.title: Objective
rules.content: Be the first to reach 30 points OR complete your secret mission with the highest score when the deck runs out.

rules.title: Card Categories
rules.content: 108 cards across 12 categories: Nature, Weather, Energy, Water, Earth, Plants, Animals, Objects, Reaction, Mission, World, and Special.

rules.title: Transform Paths
rules.content: Each World State card has transform paths (tp). You can play a card if its category matches one of the World State's transform paths.

rules.title: Turn Structure
rules.content: 1. Draw: Draw 1 card from deck. 2. Observe: Look at World State and hand. 3. Plan: Think about possible moves. 4. Transform: Play card(s) to transform World State. 5. Resolve: Wait for reactions. 6. End: Hand limit 5 (7 max after draw).

rules.title: Chain Rules
rules.content: Play multiple cards in one turn to form a chain. Each card must be playable on the previous card's transform paths. Max chain length: 5. Chain points: 1-card=1, 2-chain=1, 3-chain=3, 4-chain=6, 5-chain=10. Pure chain (all same category): 2x bonus.

rules.title: Hand Limit
rules.content: Maximum 7 cards in hand. If at 7, must play or pass. If hand is full and no playable card, player must pass.

rules.title: Reaction Cards
rules.content: Reaction cards (isReact:true) cannot be played on your own turn. They are used to respond to opponent's actions.

rules.title: Scoring
rules.content: Single transform: 1 point. 2-chain: 1 point. 3-chain: 3 points. 4-chain: 6 points. 5-chain: 10 points. Pure chain: 2x multiplier. Mission completion: 10–20 points. Successful reaction: 1 point.

rules.title: Win Conditions
rules.content: 1. 30 points reached. 2. Mission complete + highest score. 3. Deck empty (both deck and history exhausted) — highest score wins.

rules.title: Deck Management
rules.content: When deck is empty, shuffle the history pile (played cards) to form a new deck. Game ends when both deck and history are exhausted.
